message:
perf(search): hand-roll stable dedup key with pre-sized String (bead num7z)

search_hit_key_doc_id was a single format!(...) call rendering a seven-
field record joined by 0x1F. The `.map(|v| v.to_string())` branches
allocated per-field throwaway Strings for the three Option<integer>
fields (conversation_id, line_number, created_at), on top of the
one allocation the format! machinery already makes. On a rebuild that
produces ~1M dedup keys this showed as measurable heap traffic.

New shape:
  - Pre-compute a capacity estimate: sum of the three borrowed field
    lengths + 6 separators + 3*20 bytes per possibly-empty i64/usize
    + 20 bytes for the content_hash u64.
  - Push borrowed &str fields directly with String::push_str.
  - Branch on each Option at the call site — write! only if Some.
  - Final content_hash goes in via write! since it's a u64 not a &str.

The output bytes must match the previous format! rendering exactly
because this key goes into a downstream dedup HashMap — any drift
changes which hits collide across reindex cycles.

A new test search_hit_key_doc_id_matches_reference_byte_for_byte
pins the two implementations together with five fixtures covering:
  - typical populated key
  - empty strings + all-None optional fields
  - extreme integer boundaries (i64::MIN/MAX, usize::MAX, u64::MAX)
  - minimal shape with all zeros
  - embedded 0x1F separator characters in user-provided strings
    (tests that the dedup key is still parseable — six separators —
    even when the key fields contain the separator byte themselves)

Reference implementation (the old format!-based fn) is kept as
search_hit_key_doc_id_reference_v0 inside the test module so any
future accidental divergence trips CI immediately.
